promotion_rule: Strikeforce findings and mission receipts never self-promote. A bounded reviewed source change with evidence is required before a lesson becomes VERIFIED or ABSORBED.
application_rule: Every controlling lesson whose trigger matches a Sword mission must be APPLIED or classified NOT_APPLICABLE with a mission-specific reason. UNKNOWN or missing classification blocks forging.

lessons.title: Parse the launcher before mission action
lessons.failure: A PowerShell syntax defect stopped the mission before repository work began.
lessons.required_control: Parse and smoke-test the exact packaged launcher before delivery, including package-relative path resolution.

lessons.title: Seal complete candidate bytes
lessons.failure: Malformed or brittle patches failed before the intended candidate could be constructed.
lessons.required_control: Carry complete candidate bytes and hashes for additions and replacements; patches may be evidence but are not the sole payload authority.

lessons.title: Verify semantic postconditions
lessons.failure: A correct candidate was rejected by an overly broad or incorrect postcondition.
lessons.required_control: Bind postconditions to the exact intended semantic change and test them against both the source and candidate state.

lessons.title: GitHub readback is remote authority
lessons.trigger: Every operation that creates or changes GitHub state
lessons.failure: A local pull-request body comparison falsely reported failure after the remote candidate had succeeded.
lessons.required_control: Read back the exact remote branch, commit, pull request, files, and workflow state from GitHub; local serialization comparisons cannot overrule canonical readback.

lessons.title: Audit the exact final path set
lessons.trigger: Every multi-file mission and every mission containing ADD, DELETE, RENAME, or MOVE
lessons.failure: Tracked-only diff logic omitted an approved untracked ADD from the candidate audit.
lessons.required_control: Compare the exact declared operation inventory with the final GitHub tree and changed-path set; clone fallback must enumerate untracked paths before intent-to-add.

lessons.title: Treat clone behavior as a compatibility profile
lessons.trigger: Fresh Clone First or any local Git workspace is selected
lessons.failure: Windows long paths, line-ending conversion, read-only cleanup, and Git-mediated byte checks caused deterministic clone-stage failures.
lessons.required_control: Use command-scoped long-path and line-ending settings, deterministic cleanup, exact byte verification, and explicit clone-stage receipts.

lessons.title: Classify workflow applicability before waiting
lessons.trigger: Every Sword that observes or requires GitHub Actions
lessons.failure: Workflow monitoring could not distinguish required, not applicable, not appeared, running, timeout, and interrupted states.
lessons.required_control: Derive workflow applicability from exact changed paths and workflow source, then use bounded appearance and completion waits with truthful state classification.

lessons.title: Record stage truth before action
lessons.failure: Failure and interruption evidence was ambiguous when stage entry and operation state were not recorded consistently.
lessons.required_control: Enter each stage before its operation begins and emit structured success, failure, timeout, and operator-interrupt receipts with SHA-256 sidecars.

lessons.title: Bind live authority and exact heads
lessons.failure: Stale draft pull requests and historical evidence were mistaken for current authority.
lessons.required_control: Bind current canonical source, live repository identity, exact base and head, mission authority, and stop boundary immediately before construction and execution.

lessons.title: Use one executor and fast-forward repair
lessons.trigger: An existing branch or pull request is repaired
lessons.failure: Competing writers or rewritten branch history can invalidate exact-head review and receipts.
lessons.required_control: Permit one active executor per target branch, require exact-head recheck, create a single-parent child commit, and update the branch fast-forward only without force push.

lessons.title: Keep PowerShell thin and interactive
lessons.failure: Mixing source construction into the operator launcher increased platform-specific failure and obscured responsibility.
lessons.required_control: PowerShell verifies, authenticates, displays truthful stage and percentage progress, transports sealed operations, observes GitHub, and writes receipts; it does not author or reinterpret source.

lessons.title: Contain power in the pull request
lessons.failure: Self-imposed path and size guardrails risked blocking reversible construction rather than protecting permanence.
lessons.required_control: Allow arbitrary authorized clean-text multi-file construction inside the branch and pull request; enforce exact audit and separate permanence at the merge boundary.

lessons.title: Lessons never self-promote
lessons.trigger: Every Strikeforce finding or mission closeout that suggests a reusable change
lessons.failure: Unreviewed findings could silently alter future Sword behavior or create a second doctrine layer.
lessons.required_control: Keep one-off findings in receipts; promote reusable controls only through a bounded reviewed update to this register or the Forge Standard.
